key_concerns: Trial-level surrogacy did not meet pre-specified criteria (i2TEAMM primary R² ~0.4, wide CI).
Limited number of trials with sufficient MRD data, especially in transplant-eligible setting.
Heterogeneity in MRD assays and sensitivity levels across trials.
High missing MRD data rates in many studies.
No trials included CAR-T or novel therapies; uncertain generalizability.
MRD was often an exploratory endpoint, not a pre-specified key secondary endpoint.
OS data immature and confounded by subsequent therapies.
Pooling different sensitivity levels may underestimate predictive ability.
key_positives: Strong individual-level association between MRD negativity and PFS (OR >5, significant).
R² for OS in transplant-ineligible subgroup was promising (0.79–0.85).
FDA agrees methodology is reasonable and uses similar approach.
Consistent direction of treatment effect on MRD and PFS across trials.
Supportive sensitivity analyses and FDA's own pooled analysis may strengthen findings.

evidence_notes: i2TEAMM primary analysis: 'R2WLS of 0.40 (95% CI, 0.01 to 0.79) and R2Copula of 0.44 (95% CI, 0.01 to 0.88).'
Individual-level: 'global OR was generally very large, i.e. > 5.0 ... with lower bound of 95% CI >> 1.0.'
U. Miami analysis: 'R2WLS (inverse variance)=0.67 (95% CI: 0.43, 0.91)' for all NDMM PFS.
FDA: 'generally agrees with the meta-analyses conducted by both Applicants.'
i2TEAMM pre-specified threshold: 'R2WLS or R2Copula is at least 0.8 with lower bound of the 95% CI >0.6.'
FDA limitation: 'None of the trials contained in these meta-analyses include evaluation of a CAR T cell therapy.'
FDA limitation: 'The majority of the trials included did not assess 9-month or 12-month MRD rate as a key secondary endpoint.'

questions_framing.focus: adequacy of MRD negativity as a surrogate endpoint reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit for accelerated approval

document.title: April 12, 2024 Meeting of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- Combined FDA and Applicants Briefing Document

signal.label: MIXED
rationale: Concerns: Trial-level surrogacy did not meet pre-specified criteria (i2TEAMM primary R² ~0.4, wide CI).; Limited number of trials with sufficient MRD data, especially in transplant-eligible setting.; Heterogeneity in MRD assays and sensitivity levels across trials.; High missing MRD data rates in many studies.; No trials included CAR-T or novel therapies; uncertain generalizability.

Positives: Strong individual-level association between MRD negativity and PFS (OR >5, significant).; R² for OS in transplant-ineligible subgroup was promising (0.79–0.85).; FDA agrees methodology is reasonable and uses similar approach.; Consistent direction of treatment effect on MRD and PFS across trials.; Supportive sensitivity analyses and FDA's own pooled analysis may strengthen findings.
